title: cordylanthus kingii subsp. helleri
intro: Cordylanthus kingii subsp. helleri, also known as Heller's bird's beak, is a perennial wildflower native to the Pacific coast of North America, specifically found in California and Oregon. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which can help to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. The plant's leaves and stems are rich in flavonoids, glycosides, and terpenoids, providing various health benefits such as antioxidant, anti-viral, and immune-boosting effects. In herbal preparations, Heller's bird's beak can be used as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, often combined with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic properties.
